headline: BTS's Seoul Homecoming Boosts City Economy While Netflix Claims Crown of Live Concert Streaming
summary: On Saturday, BTS took the stage in central Seoul for a large-scale comeback show after a four-year pause, with the performance carried live on Netflix and built around the group's new album rollout.

headline: Bessent Defends Iran Escalation Amid Soaring Oil Prices
summary: On Sunday, Scott Bessent defended U.S. strikes on Iranian infrastructure as part of the U.S.-Israel war that began in February, arguing the campaign requires escalation before tensions can cool.

headline: President Trump, Speaking To Israel's News 13 Regarding The Ultimatum On Iran's Power Plants, Said The Result Would Be "Excellent."
Schumer Warns About Trump's Provocations: 'The Cruelty Is The Point'
summary: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer has condemned President Donald Trump for a social media remark celebrating the death of former special counsel Robert Mueller, while urging the public not to get pulled into what he framed as a diversion.

headline: Survey: 90% Of Advisors Say AI Will Redefine – Not Replace – Their Role
summary: Advisors embrace AI for efficiency, but not decision-making. Surveys show clients expect more personalized advice while AI handles tasks, yet tech limitations hinder its potential.

headline: Bill Ackman on Treasury's Fannie/Freddie Deal: 'Now That You Have Time, Mr. President, Let's Stop the Steal!'
summary: Bill Ackman blasted the U.S. Treasury's handling of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac on Sunday, arguing the government rewrote the rescue terms through the Net Worth Sweep and diverted all quarterly profits to itself even after the companies had already repaid the bailout.

headline: Steve Jobs Once Brushed Away The Where Do You See Yourself In 10-Years Question: 'My Headlights Are Not That Good'
summary: Jobs believed in shorter planning horizons for fast-moving technology companies, as long-term plans often become obsolete. But he also valued a longer-term view for building a team.

headline: Trump: 'The Greatest Enemy America Has Is the Radical Left,' Targets Talarico and Newsom
summary: On Sunday, Donald Trump used the Truth Social post to argue that the Democratic Party's "Radical Left" is now the country's top adversary, after what he described as the "death of Iran."

headline: Iran: Strait of Hormuz Open, But Not for 'Iran's Enemies'
summary: On Sunday, Iranian media cited Iran's representative to the U.N. maritime agency as saying the Strait of Hormuz would remain available for commercial traffic, with an exception for vessels tied to Iran's enemies.

headline: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Announced Energy Facilities In Countries Hosting U.S. Bases Would Be Lawful Targets If Trump Strikes Iran Energy Facilities, Warned Companies With U.S. Shares Would Be Completely Destroyed - Reuters Cited IRGC Statement
Naval Ravikant Once Revealed What Advice He Would Give His 20-Year Old Self: Here's What The Veteran Twitter And Uber Investor Had To Say
summary: Naval Ravikant, a well-known venture capitalist and influencer, once stated that when he looks back on his life, he has only one piece of advice for his younger self — relax and don’t stress.

headline: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Warn Strait Of Hormuz Will Be Completely Closed If Trump Targets Iran Energy Facilities -Reuters

CORRECTION: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Announced Energy Facilities In Countries Hosting U.S. Bases Would Be Lawful Targets If Trump Strikes Iran Energy Facilities, Warned Companies With U.S. Shares Would Be Completely Destroyed
Scaramucci Warns Iran Conflict Could Force Fed Into Rapid Rate Hikes, Says 'Trump Cannot Believe His Luck Has Run Out in Iran'
summary: Anthony Scaramucci has warned that the Iran conflict could flip the Federal Reserve from preparing rate cuts to considering faster hikes, arguing war-driven inflation expectations are already shifting.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Bitcoin Holds $70,000, Ethereum's 'Buy Zone', Nasdaq's Blockchain Stocks, SEC's Crypto Framework And More
summary: The week in crypto saw Bitcoin hold near $70,000 as Ethereum entered a "buy zone," while Nasdaq, Inc. gained SEC approval for tokenized stocks, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission unveiled a new crypto framework, and Morgan Stanley advanced its Bitcoin ETF plans.

headline: Kevin O'Leary: Hormuz Crisis Will Get 'Cleaned Up In The Next 30 Days'
summary: Kevin O'Leary on Saturday said that investors are pricing in a quick resolution to tensions tied to the Strait of Hormuz, arguing the next month is the key window the market is watching.

headline: Trump Proposes Shifting ICE Agents To Airports
summary: On Saturday, President Donald Trump said he could shift ICE agents into U.S. airports to take over security duties and carry out on-the-spot detentions of people in the country illegally, singling out immigrants from Somalia.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Rivian's Uber Deal, Gerber's EV Push, Canada's Auto Strategy, Tesla's Battery Plant And United Airlines' Flight Cuts
summary: The week in business and tech saw Rivian Automotive strike a $1.25 billion robotaxi deal with Uber Technologies, investor Ross Gerber push EV adoption amid rising oil prices, Canada propose a U.S.-focused auto strategy, Tesla, Inc. plan a $4.3 billion battery plant, and United Airlines cut flights due to higher fuel costs.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Palantir's Pentagon Win, Nvidia's Trillion-Dollar Vision, Super Micro's DOJ Woes And Musk's Legal Setback
summary: The week in tech saw Palantir Technologies secure a major Pentagon AI contract win, NVIDIA outline a $1 trillion revenue vision, Super Micro Computer face DOJ-related setbacks, Micron Technology post strong Q2 earnings, and Elon Musk hit with a jury ruling over the Twitter deal.

headline: Elon Musk Unveils Ambitious Terafab Plans For Tesla, SpaceX: 'We're Starting A Galactic Civilization'
summary: Elon Musk unveiled plans for the Terafab project, a joint venture between Tesla and SpaceX imed at transforming chip manufacturing.

headline: Apple's Best-Ever Mac Launch, Google's Gemini On Mac, Amazon's 'Transformer' Project And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Weekend tech and finance highlights saw Apple's new Mac launch break records, Google tests a Gemini AI app, and Amazon develops an AI-driven smartphone project.

headline: Trump's Iran War Sparks Bond Market Revolt, Gold's Worst Week Since 1983 And More: This Week In Economy
summary: Weekend market turmoil saw Trump's Iran war influence interest rate bets, spark outrage over a White House adviser's remarks, and trigger gold's worst week since 1983.

headline: Trump Refuses To Negotiate, Malaysia's Bold Move And More: This Week In Politics
summary: Weekend headlines saw Malaysia reject a U.S. trade deal and Trump issue stern warnings on Iran, while Warren questioned Amazon's Melania Trump documentary spending.

headline: OpenAI Plans To Nearly Double Workforce Amid AI Race With Anthropic, Google
summary: OpenAI plans to double its workforce and expand its AI offerings amid growing competition and major funding backing.

headline: Box CEO Flags Soaring AI Costs As Usage Expands Beyond Engineers
summary: Tech companies struggle with rising costs of AI tools, affecting not just engineering teams but also legal and sales.

headline: Japan Weighs Hormuz Minesweeping If Ceasefire Reached In Iran War: Report
summary: Japan may use military for minesweeping in Strait of Hormuz if a ceasefire is reached in the U.S.-Iran war.

headline: Andrej Karpathy Says AI Agents Are Rewriting How Software Gets Built, Adds He Hasn't Typed 'A Line Of Code Probably Since December,'
summary: Andrej Karpathy says AI agents have rapidly reshaped software development, with top experts struggling to keep up.

headline: Exclusive: Expert Weighs In On Why Orbital Datacenters Could Likely Work In Tandem With Ground-Based AI Infrastructure
summary: Space-based AI compute has potential to solve issues on Earth but it faces challenges, according to this expert.

headline: Iran Threatens To Target Critical Infrastructure If US Strikes Power Plants
summary: Iran warns it will strike energy and desalination sites if the U.S. attacks its power plants, heightening tensions at the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: Bernie Sanders Warns Jeff Bezos' $100 Billion Robot Push Will 'Replace Working People,' Urges Americans To 'Stand Up And FIGHT BACK'
summary: Bernie Sanders warns that Jeff Bezos's $100 billion automation plan could devastate jobs. Other experts and lawmakers also express concerns.

headline: Mark Cuban Says It's 'Gonna Get Crazy' As AI Transforms Business, Calls For Vendor Trust System
summary: Mark Cuban warns that AI agents could reshape U.S. markets, urging a trust system for vendors to manage the new digital economy.

headline: 'Donald Trump Doesn't Like To Lose,' Says Slotkin As Democrats Warn SAVE America Act Could Suppress Millions Of Votes
summary: Democrats criticize Trump's SAVE Act as voter suppression to give him an unfair advantage in the 2026 election.

headline: Mark Cuban Says AI Revolution Is Easier For White Collar Workers Than PC Revolution, Predicts Job Cuts At Big Companies
summary: Mark Cuban warned that workers must quickly adopt AI skills to stay competitive as technology reshapes jobs.

headline: Trump Says 'They Want To Make A Deal. I Don't' Amid Iran War, Dismisses Reports About Missing Goals
summary: Trump rejects Iran's offer to negotiate despite their willingness. Says US has achieved its objectives and Iran has no defense.

headline: Ted Cruz Pushes Separate ICE, CBP Funding Amid Airport Chaos As Government Shutdown Stretches TSA Lines To Four Hours
summary: Sen. Ted Cruz proposed funding ICE and CBP separately to end the shutdown. Elon Musk offered to cover TSA paychecks during the funding lapse.

headline: Trump Issues 48-Hour Ultimatum To Iran Over Strait Of Hormuz, Threatens Power Plant Strikes
summary: President Donald Trump warned Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or face U.S. strikes on its power plants.
